denial_pattern_sources: non_specialist_reviewer: MHPAEA Final Rule 2024 (29 CFR § 2590.712); Cal. Health & Safety Code § 1374.72
step_therapy_vague_mcg: KFF 2023 Survey of Consumer Experiences with Health Insurance
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 01/07/2026 for Letrozole ovulation induction related to Polycystic ovary syndrome with infertility (E28.2). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. A lower level of care is appropriate for Polycystic ovary syndrome with infertility (E28.2); Letrozole ovulation induction exceeds what is required. Outpatient management has not been exhausted per clinical guidelines.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and utilization-management requirements associated with level of care too high. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management

This determination was made by Dr. J. Smith, Medical Director.
clinical_context: The 19-year-old member has Polycystic ovary syndrome with infertility (E28.2). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Letrozole ovulation induction recommended as the next appropriate step. Anovulatory cycles; failed clomiphene 100 mg ×3 cycles. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that level of care too high bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings (including dated scale scores in the chart) that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
